authoritySource: Dr. Ahmed Hesham — OET Nursing Vocabulary Rulebook v1

rules.title: Definitions clinically accurate
rules.body: Definitions reflect current nursing-process and evidence-based practice. No outdated nursing theory language ('handicapped', 'patient complains').

rules.title: No brand names
rules.body: Primary terms use generic medication or device names. Brand names only as secondary synonyms.

rules.title: Abbreviations expanded
rules.body: Every abbreviation term includes its expansion.

rules.title: Nursing handover register
rules.body: Entries must read as they would in a SBAR / ISBAR nursing handover or nursing care plan.

rules.title: No trademarked names
rules.body: Never trademarked devices as primary terms.

rules.title: Person-centred language
rules.body: Use 'patient with diabetes' not 'diabetic patient'; 'patient living with dementia' not 'demented'.

rules.title: No circular definitions
rules.body: A definition must not repeat the term.

rules.title: Example sentence in nursing context
rules.body: Authentic nursing sentence (handover, care-plan, medication round, discharge). 8–30 words.

rules.title: Synonyms genuine
rules.body: English nursing synonyms only. No translations.

rules.title: Canonical categories
rules.body: One of: medical, anatomy, symptoms, procedures, pharmacology, conditions, clinical_communication, diagnostics, nursing_care.

rules.title: Example sentence passes core grammar
rules.body: No contractions; 'the patient' not a running label; Latin abbreviations translated.

rules.title: Balanced nursing bands
rules.body: Bank covers: nursing assessment, care planning, medication administration, wound care, patient education, handover language.
